alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the preprint's description (privacy-preserving, constant-iteration oracle) with a specific geometric-median-based algorithm and efficient computation, changing technical details and not mentioning the privacy/iteration claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robust aggregation approach outperforms the classical aggregation approach in terms of robustness when the level of corruption is high.

alignments.rationale: The publication removes the specific baseline number and frames the comparison more generally against other neural networks, broadening the scope and revising the specific numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments verified the effectiveness of random shift wavelet and expressway structure in the optical diffractive network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random shift wavelets exhibit characteristics of optical networks, particularly addressing the vanishing gradient phenomenon during training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a particular mechanism (addressing vanishing gradients) rather than the broader experimental verification claim, so the result is revised and narrowed to a specific observed characteristic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning from an imbalanced dataset is one of the most important practical problems in the field of machine learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not repeat the general statement about importance of imbalanced learning; instead it focuses on the proposed methods and results, so this general claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A combination of a weighted loss function and batch normalization leads to a size-inconsistency problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of WLF and batch normalization (BN) leads to a size-inconsistency problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The technical result about size-inconsistency is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple modification to batch normalization, called weighted batch normalization, is proposed to correct the size mismatch. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A modification to BN, called weighted BN (WBN), is proposed to correct the size mismatch. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution (WBN) is the same in both versions with equivalent description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method in a data-imbalanced environment is validated using numerical experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, WBN, is validated using numerical experiments in a data-imbalanced environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental validation claim is retained without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A weighted loss function (WLF) is an effective method for addressing imbalanced datasets in machine learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit endorsement of WLF as an effective method, which was not present as a standalone claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed modified syndrome losses facilitate unsupervised learning in the receiver for polar codes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed modified syndrome losses facilitate unsupervised learning in the receiver for polar codes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BP decoder with CRC-enabled syndrome loss outperforms conventional supervised learning methods in terms of block error rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network-based belief propagation (BP) polar decoder with CRC-enabled syndrome loss outperforms conventional supervised learning methods in terms of block error rate. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The jointly optimized syndrome-enabled blind equalizer achieves a global optimum with a 1.3 dB gain over the non-blind minimum mean square error (MMSE) equalizer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The jointly optimized syndrome-enabled blind equalizer achieves a global optimum with a 1.3 dB gain over the non-blind minimum mean square error (MMSE) equalizer. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep transfer learning method is proposed to address challenges in automatically interpreting SAR images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep transfer learning method demonstrates high efficiency in transferring information from a similarly annotated remote sensing data set. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates high efficiency in transferring information from a similarly annotated remote sensing dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep transfer learning method demonstrates high efficiency in transferring information from a similarly annotated remote sensing data set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This empirical transfer-efficiency claim appears in both versions with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method shows robust performance on highly imbalanced classes and alleviates the over-fitting problem caused by label noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method shows robust performance on highly imbalanced classes and alleviates the overfitting problem caused by label noise. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned deep model has a good generalization for other SAR-specific tasks, achieving a state-of-the-art classification accuracy of 99.46% in MSTAR target recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a relationship between model complexity and the effort required for users to exert control over their recommendations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a relationship between model complexity and the effort necessary for users to exert control over their recommendations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our insights provide a novel perspective on the user cold-start problem in recommender systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work provides a novel perspective on the user cold-start problem.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Secml enables the evaluation of the security of learning algorithms under both white-box and black-box threat models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Secml provides built-in functions to compute security evaluation curves that show the decrease in classification performance against increasing adversarial perturbations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The library includes explainability methods to visualize the most influential features and training prototypes contributing to each decision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing decomposition methods fail to capture the common pattern between the two coefficient matrices of the common latent factors.
